name: Add or Replace a Roommate on the Lease
description: Call the landlord or property manager — as the master-leaseholder — to request adding a new roommate, replacing a departing roommate, or formalising an existing informal subletter, while preserving the existing lease terms and the master-leaseholder's deposit posture.
disclaimer: Not legal advice. Habitability + early-termination skills are scripted to PRESERVE legal posture (request things in writing, refuse verbal-only assurances) — but the operator must consult a tenant attorney for actual rights determination.

context.when_to_use: User is the named tenant on a lease and wants to: add a new roommate mid-lease, replace one roommate with another, or convert an informal arrangement into a written addendum. Use BEFORE the new roommate moves in — moving them in without permission can trigger lease violation and eviction.
preconditions: User has the current lease and knows the clauses on additional occupants / sublet / assignment.
User has the prospective roommate's full legal name, contact info, and consent for a background / credit check.
User knows the current rent split arrangement and whether it changes when the roommate joins.
User has a clear ask: ADD (lease stays, new person added), REPLACE (old roommate off lease, new on), or FORMALISE (existing informal occupant added retroactively).

principles: Most leases let the landlord screen new occupants but cannot unreasonably withhold consent — know the lease language before the call.
Keep the existing lease terms intact. Landlords sometimes try to 'reset' the lease (raise rent, change duration) when a roommate change is requested. Push back: it's an addendum, not a new lease.
Get the new roommate added to the lease, not just verbally approved. Verbal approval doesn't bind the landlord and doesn't give the new occupant tenant rights.
Clarify deposit treatment up front. Adding a roommate sometimes requires additional deposit; replacing one shouldn't.
If a roommate is leaving and money is owed (rent, deposit), settle that BETWEEN roommates before involving the landlord. Landlords stay out of roommate-to-roommate accounting.
Background-check fees are normal; demand for excessive new deposit or a rent bump is not.

phrases.opener_add: Hi, I'm the named tenant at [unit, address]. I'd like to add a roommate to the lease — what's the process and timeline?
phrases.opener_replace: Hi, one of my roommates is moving out at [date] and I'd like to replace them with a new co-tenant. What's the process for swapping them on the lease?
phrases.opener_formalize: Hi, I have someone currently staying with me and I'd like to get them added to the lease formally. What does that look like?
phrases.ask_for_lease_continuity: I want to make sure this is an addendum to my current lease — same rent, same end date — not a new lease. Can you confirm?
phrases.ask_about_screening: What's the screening process for the new roommate? Application fee, credit, income requirements — what should they expect?
phrases.ask_about_deposit: Will any additional deposit be required, and if so, who pays it and when?
phrases.push_back_on_terms_change: I'd like to keep the current rent and lease end date as is — the addendum is just adding a person, not renegotiating. Is that workable?
phrases.ask_for_addendum_in_writing: Once the screening is done, can you send the addendum by email so we can both review before signing?
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. Next steps: [roommate name] will submit [application/fee/documents] by [date], you'll send the addendum by [date], we sign by [date]. Anything else I should send?

tactics.name: Open with the specific ask
tactics.script: Use the matching opener — ADD, REPLACE, or FORMALIZE. Each has different downstream implications; the landlord needs to know which.

tactics.name: Anchor on lease continuity
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_lease_continuity`. Locks the conversation as an addendum, not a re-negotiation. This is the single most important defensive move.

tactics.name: Walk the screening process
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_screening`. Get the application fee, the documents needed, the turnaround. Pass this to the prospective roommate before they apply.

tactics.name: Clarify deposit treatment
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_deposit`. ADD may require additional deposit; REPLACE generally shouldn't (it transfers liability). FORMALIZE is in between.

tactics.name: Push back on rent / term increases
tactics.when: Landlord proposes a new rent or new lease term as a condition.
tactics.script: Use `push_back_on_terms_change`. Adding a roommate is not a re-leasing event. If landlord insists on a new lease, escalate to `ask_operator` — that's a renegotiation, not an addendum.

tactics.name: Lock the addendum in writing
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_addendum_in_writing`. Do not consider the new roommate approved until the addendum is signed by all parties.

boundaries: Do NOT move the new roommate in before the addendum is signed.
Do NOT agree to a higher rent or a longer lease as a condition of adding a roommate unless the user has explicitly authorised it.
Do NOT agree to release the departing roommate from rent liability without a signed substitution. The landlord controls release; one tenant cannot release another.
Do NOT share the prospective roommate's SSN, income, or background-check results on the phone — those go through the formal application channel.
Do NOT take on the prospective roommate's screening risk (e.g. agreeing to cover their share if they default) without operator review.
success_signals: Landlord agrees to keep lease terms intact and process via addendum.
Clear screening process with a known fee and timeline.
Addendum will be sent by email for review before signing.
Replacement: departing roommate formally released on signature.
failure_signals: Landlord insists on a brand-new lease at higher rent.
Landlord refuses to add anyone (check lease language — sometimes lawful, sometimes not).
Landlord demands additional deposit beyond normal screening fees with no documentation.
Landlord won't put the addendum in writing.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm screening process, addendum delivery date, sign-by date, and that lease terms are unchanged. Email the prospective roommate the application instructions.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Do NOT move the roommate in. If the landlord is unreasonable, review the lease's 'additional occupant' clause with a tenant attorney; in some jurisdictions consent cannot be unreasonably withheld.
follow_ups: Send the prospective roommate the screening requirements.
Settle any roommate-to-roommate money (rent, shared deposit) BEFORE the addendum signature.
Calendar the addendum delivery date and the sign-by date.
After signing, confirm with the bank / utilities which accounts need a name change.

required_user_info: Current lease and clauses on additional occupants / assignment / sublet
Prospective roommate's full legal name and contact info
If REPLACE: departing roommate's move-out date and any money owed between roommates
Whether the user is willing to take on additional deposit or to absorb the new roommate's risk
